commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9b034b825c5dd3b34b04bf0d1ef77d720f81a7d4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex app-server final-answer candidate lifecycle events and tests that earlier candidates become superseded, the selected final answer remains the reply payload, and suppressed candidate item events do not reach channel item progress.

PR surface: Source +131, Tests +184. Total +315 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source trace: the PR emits item answer-candidate events, while current Control UI activity handling ignores non-tool streams and the activity model is tool-call keyed. I did not run the UI.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs author or maintainer follow-up because automation cannot supply the contributor's real behavior proof, and the UI activity contract needs an explicit repair before merge.

target.title: Surface Codex final answer candidates in activity
target.body:
## Summary

- Emit dashboard/activity `item` events for Codex `phase: "final_answer"` answer candidates.
- Mark earlier candidates as `superseded` and the terminal selected candidate as `selected` while preserving Codex native history and existing partial streaming.
- Keep answer-candidate activity out of channel progress by setting `suppressChannelProgress: true`; final channel delivery still uses the selected final reply payload.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server can stream a `phase: "final_answer"` candidate, then continue with tool work, then produce a later final answer. The native candidate history is real and should remain visible to operator/debug UIs, but messaging channels should still project one authoritative final answer rather than surfacing candidate activity as normal channel progress.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw repository on macOS, branch `agent/xiaozhua/codex-final-answer-candidates`, using the repository Vitest wrapper and the existing Codex app-server/event-projector and auto-reply pipeline tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts --testNamePattern="records superseded and selected final-answer candidates without changing the final reply"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --testNamePattern="keeps suppressed answer candidates out of channel item progress"
git diff --check
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Focused synthetic Codex stream test constructs `final_answer candidate ("Draft answer") → commandExecution tool → final_answer ("Final answer")` and verifies the terminal projection plus dashboard/activity events:

```text
result.assistantTexts == ["Final answer"]
onPartialReply receives the streamed candidate text and later final text
activity item events include:
  msg-early-final:    status=candidate   progressText="Draft answer" suppressChannelProgress=true
  msg-early-final:    status=superseded  progressText="Draft answer" suppressChannelProgress=true
  msg-selected-final: status=candidate   progressText="Final answer" suppressChannelProgress=true
  msg-selected-final: status=selected    progressText="Final answer" suppressChannelProgress=true
```

The channel-pipeline test verifies suppressed answer-candidate item events do not call `onItemEvent` while the run result payload remains:

```text
[{ text: "Final answer" }]
```

Full related test file output:

```text
extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts: 79 passed
src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts: 183 passed
oxlint: Found 0 warnings and 0 errors
```

Observed result after fix: Dashboard/activity subscribers can observe two Codex answer candidates for the `final_answer → tool → final_answer` stream, with the earlier candidate marked `superseded` and the final one marked `selected`. Channel progress callbacks suppress those answer-candidate activity records, and the selected final answer remains the only final reply payload.

What was not tested: No live Telegram bot message was sent from this PR branch. The channel guarantee is covered at the auto-reply pipeline boundary that Telegram uses for item-progress callbacks and final reply payloads. Raw Codex native event persistence is unchanged by this patch.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts --testNamePattern="records superseded and selected final-answer candidates without changing the final reply"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --testNamePattern="keeps suppressed answer candidates out of channel item progress"`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
